input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given inputs question (string) and passages (list of strings), produce a single textual output to populate the field "summary". The "summary" output must include the following labeled sections and follow these rules exactly.

1) Format
- Begin with: [[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- End the reasoning block and then include: [[ ## summary ## ]]
- The final output may optionally end with: [[ ## completed ## ]]

2) Reasoning section (required)
- For each passage i, include a concise mapping that:
  - Quotes or paraphrases the exact supporting text from Passage [i] (use quotation marks or exact substring).
  - Explicitly maps each quoted fact to the entity and scope it supports (e.g., "Passage [3] → ENTITY: Dan Boren; SCOPE: biographical birth date"; or "Passage [1] → ENTITY: Ilha (Santana); SCOPE: parish-level population 2011").
  - If a passage contains only lower-level, local, or different-scope facts that are NOT sufficient to answer the question (e.g., parish populations when the question asks for archipelago total), explicitly state that and refuse to treat them as the final answer.

- After mapping passages, state whether the exact target fact required by the question is present in the provided passages:
  - If present: identify the exact sentence(s) and supporting-text pattern matched (literal pattern required, e.g., "ENTITY_NAME (born <date>)" or "The population in 2011 was <number>"). Cite passage indices that contain that exact supporting text.
  - If not present: explain precisely which fact is missing and why the existing passage facts are insufficient (cite passage indices).

3) If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (required when exact supporting text is absent)
- Name the target entity to retrieve (canonical/title-first form) and give one-sentence rationale linking it to the question (use the bridging passage index).
- Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants (each a short search phrase). At least one variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion. Include scope/context tokens (e.g., "birth date", "2011 population", "archipelago", "U.S. Representative", "politician", location).
- Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern, e.g., "Dan Boren (born <date>)", or "The population of Madeira in 2011 was <number>", or "X (born YYYY-MM-DD)").
- Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Dan Boren" + "Oklahoma's 2nd Congressional District" or "Ilha (Santana) → Madeira").
- Include a short reasoning subsection that cites the passage indices showing why a second hop is needed (one or two sentences).

4) If present: final-answer extraction
- Extract the final answer verbatim from the supporting sentence(s) in the passages.
- State the exact supporting-text pattern matched.
- Cite the passage index(es) that contain the supporting text.

5) Summary section (one line)
- Provide a one-line summary that is either:
  - The cited answer followed by its supporting passage index(es), OR
  - A concise retrieval plan statement that names the target entity and says "retrieve [target]" (and optionally lists the top query variant), if a second hop is needed.
- Do not include any new facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.

6) Constraints and behavior
- Never answer from memory. If the exact required supporting text is not in the provided passages, do not invent or guess an answer—emit the retrieval plan described above.
- Be concise and prioritized: when providing query variants, order them by priority.
- Always include passage indices in reasoning to justify conclusions.
- Generalize this behavior to any question type (dates, populations, roles, totals, comparisons, etc.).

delta_p.trigger: When the passages attribute the song to a named artist (the artist's name appears in connection with the song) but none of the passages contain an explicit literal membership-status phrase linking that artist to the target band (no exact strings such as "former member", "founding member", "member" together with the band name, or membership years for that artist with the band).
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep all existing mapping and citation behavior from the base prompt: quote or paraphrase per passage with passage-index citations; preserve the required output format tokens ([[ ## reasoning ## ]], [[ ## summary ## ]] and optional [[ ## completed ## ]]); do not change the output field name or interface; continue to refuse to answer from memory.
delta_p.change_rule: If the trigger condition holds (artist attribution present but explicit membership-status text absent), insert a concise 'Bridge / missing fact' sentence in the reasoning that names the missing fact the next hop must verify (e.g., whether <Artist> is/was a member of <Band>). Then produce a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan targeted at the artist's canonical biography page (canonical/title-first form, e.g., 'Don Henley'). The retrieval plan must include: (a) one-sentence rationale linking the target biography to the question using the bridging passage index; (b) at least three short query variants ordered by priority, with the first variant beginning with the canonical/title-first suggestion; (c) the exact literal supporting-text pattern(s) required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (acceptable literal patterns include one of: "Don Henley is a former member of the Eagles", "Don Henley is a member of the Eagles", "Don Henley is a founding member of the Eagles", or a membership-line such as "Member of the Eagles (YYYY–YYYY)"); (d) exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Don Henley" + "The End of the Innocence" or "Don Henley" + "Eagles"); and (e) a one- or two-sentence reasoning subsection citing the passage index(es) that show why the second hop is needed. Do not assert or output the final membership status here—only request retrieval that would supply one of the literal patterns above.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assert that the artist is a 'former' or current member of the band unless an exact supporting-text pattern (one of the literal patterns listed above) appears in the provided passages. Avoid inventing membership words or dates. Do not output the final answer or copy any hidden gold labels. Do not alter the required formatting or omit passage citations.
delta_p.output_contract: The modified prompt must still produce a single 'summary' output that follows the base-format rules. When artist attribution is present but membership-status is not explicit, the reasoning must (1) map passage evidence as before, (2) include a short 'Bridge / missing fact' sentence naming the exact membership fact to verify, and (3) include the prioritized retrieval plan with query variants, required literal supporting-text pattern(s), bridging phrases, and short reasoning citing passage indices. The final one-line summary must either extract the answer (only if exact literal supporting text is present) or state 'retrieve [Target]' with the top query variant.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The source output correctly identifies the artist but omits an explicit statement that the artist's membership status with the Eagles is unverified; this small rule addition forces the agent to expose that missing bridge fact and to produce a concrete next-hop retrieval plan targeted at the artist biography so the pipeline can complete the multi-hop verification without guessing.

visible_agent_inputs.question: The End of the Innocence was a single by which former member of the Eagles?
passages: The End of the Innocence (song) | "The End of the Innocence" is the lead single and title track from Don Henley's third solo studio album of the same name, released in 1989.  Henley co-wrote and co-produced the song with Bruce Hornsby, who also performed piano; both artists perform the song live in their respective concerts.  Henley's version peaked at number eight on the "Billboard" Hot 100, becoming his fifth solo top ten hit on the chart, more than any of the other members of The Eagles.  "The End of the Innocence" also became his fourth number-one single on the Mainstream Rock Tracks chart and peaked at number two on the Hot Adult Contemporary chart.  The song features Wayne Shorter on saxophone.

The End of the Innocence (album) | The End of the Innocence is the third solo studio album by Don Henley, the lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles.  The album was released in 1989, on Geffen Records, and would be his last release on that label.  It was also his last solo album of the 1980s and it would be eleven years before he released another solo album, until 2000's "Inside Job".

Innocent (Stereophonics song) | "Innocent" is a single by British rock band Stereophonics which was released on 9 November 2009.  It was the first single taken from their seventh studio album, "Keep Calm and Carry On", which was released a week later, on 16 November 2009.  It was also the band's first single for Mercury Records.

Sweet and Innocent (Diamond Head song) | Sweet and Innocent is a single by heavy metal band Diamond Head released in 1980 by Media Records, a Wolverhampton-based label.  It was a single A-side "Streets of Gold" as the B-Side, and was only available on 7".  Both tracks eventually ended up on the re-released version of Diamond Head's 1980 debut Lightning to the Nations in 2001 by Sanctuary Records.

Moving Pictures (band) | Moving Pictures were an Australian rock music band formed in 1978.  Their debut album, "Days of Innocence", was issued in October 1981 and eventually peaked at No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Albums Chart in February the following year.  In January 1982 they released their single, "What About Me", which reached No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Singles Chart.  Late that year Elektra Records issued "Days of Innocence" and "What About Me" in North America.  The single reached No. 29 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and appeared on the associated year-end Hot 100 list for 1983.  A proposed series of United States performances supporting REO Speedwagon, Tom Petty and Hall & Oates fell through when Elektra was substantially reorganised.

The Last Worthless Evening | "The Last Worthless Evening" is a song written by John Corey, Don Henley, and Stan Lynch.  It was a single recorded by Henley in 1989 which reached #21 on US charts.  The song was included on Henley's third album "The End of the Innocence" that same year.
Innocent Chinyoka | Innocent Chinyoka (born 21 June 1982) is a Zimbabwean first-class cricketer who plays for Mashonaland Eagles.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

response:
{
  "output": "[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Passage [1] → quoted: "'The End of the Innocence' is the lead single and title track from Don Henley's third solo studio album... Henley's version peaked at number eight on the 'Billboard' Hot 100... more than any of the other members of The Eagles." → ENTITY: Don Henley; SCOPE: attribution of the song to Don Henley and his solo chart performance (ties the song to Henley).
Passage [2] → quoted: "The End of the Innocence is the third solo studio album by Don Henley, the lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles." → ENTITY: Don Henley; SCOPE: states Henley's role/association with the Eagles (role phrasing present but does not contain an explicit literal 'former member' or exact membership-line pattern required for final verification).
Passage [3] → quoted/paraphrase: "'Innocent' is a single by British rock band Stereophonics..." → ENTITY: Stereophonics; SCOPE: unrelated single (not relevant to the question about The End of the Innocence or the Eagles).
Passage [4] → quoted/paraphrase: "Sweet and Innocent is a single by heavy metal band Diamond Head..." → ENTITY: Diamond Head; SCOPE: unrelated single (insufficient for this question).
Passage [5] → quoted/paraphrase: "Moving Pictures were an Australian rock music band... their single 'What About Me'..." → ENTITY: Moving Pictures; SCOPE: unrelated band/single (insufficient).
Passage [6] → quoted/paraphrase: "'The Last Worthless Evening' is a song written by John Corey, Don Henley, and Stan Lynch... a single recorded by Henley in 1989... included on Henley's third album 'The End of the Innocence'." → ENTITY: Don Henley; SCOPE: songwriting/recording credit tying Henley to material on the album (supports Henley as performer/author of tracks on the album, not membership status).
Passage [7] → quoted/paraphrase: "Innocent Chinyoka (born 21 June 1982) is a Zimbabwean first-class cricketer who plays for Mashonaland Eagles." → ENTITY: Innocent Chinyoka; SCOPE: unrelated person/team (not relevant to the music question).

Exact target fact present? No. The question asks for the single's performer described as a "former member of the Eagles." None of the passages contain the exact literal membership-status pattern required (e.g., "Don Henley is a former member of the Eagles", "Don Henley is a member of the Eagles", "Don Henley is a founding member of the Eagles", or a membership-line such as "Member of the Eagles (YYYY–YYYY)"). Passage [2] does state Henley's role "lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles" and passage [1] attributes the single to Henley, but neither passage contains one of the exact literal supporting-text patterns that would permit answering the question as phrased (no explicit "former" membership pattern or membership-line is present). Cite: passages [1], [2].

Bridge / missing fact: Whether Don Henley is/was a (specifically "former") member of the Eagles — the next hop must verify Don Henley's membership-status wording.

Prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (target: Don Henley):
- Target entity: Don Henley. Rationale: Passage [1] attributes the single to Don Henley and Passage [2] links Henley to the Eagles, so retrieve Don Henley's canonical biography to confirm the exact membership-status wording (see passages [1], [2]).
- Priority query variants (ordered):
  1) "Don Henley former member of the Eagles"
  2) "Don Henley is a former member of the Eagles"
  3) "Don Henley member of the Eagles biography"
  4) "Don Henley Eagles membership years"
  (Each variant should include scope tokens such as: "former member", "Eagles", "membership", or "The End of the Innocence" as needed.)
- Exact supporting-text pattern required from retrieved document (literal patterns that will permit a final answer): one of
  - "Don Henley is a former member of the Eagles"
  - "Don Henley is a member of the Eagles"
  - "Don Henley is a founding member of the Eagles"
  - a membership-line such as: "Member of the Eagles (YYYY–YYYY)"
- Exact bridging phrases the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target: e.g., "Don Henley" + "The End of the Innocence"; and "Don Henley" + "Eagles".
- Short reasoning for second hop: Passage [1] attributes the single to Don Henley and passage [2] associates Henley with the Eagles, but neither contains the exact literal membership-status phrase (e.g., the word "former" or a membership-line). Therefore retrieve Don Henley's biography page to locate an explicit membership statement (cite passages [1], [2]).

[[ ## summary ## ]]
retrieve Don Henley (top query: "Don Henley former member of the Eagles")
[[ ## completed ## ]]",
  "rationale": "Applied the base prompt mapping rules to each passage and the ordered delta: detected that artist attribution is present but no exact literal 'former member' membership pattern appears, inserted a Bridge/missing-fact sentence, and produced a prioritized Don Henley biography retrieval plan with required literal patterns and queries."
}
